# Introduction

This course introduces students to the core practices of experimental science, including formulating scientific questions, designing and carrying out experiments, maintaining a laboratory notebook, interpreting results, and presenting findings. These are the practical skills needed to work efficiently in a research laboratory, regardless of discipline.

Recommended background: Prior or current enrollment in MB 352 or MB 354.

## 🎯 Course Outcomes
Upon completion of this course, students will be able to:
CO 1: Formulate meaningful and testable scientific questions and hypotheses
CO 2: Design experimental tests to answer scientific questions, including appropriate controls and variable manipulation
CO 3: Create detailed experimental plans
CO 4: Design and prepare all materials required to carry out an experimental plan
CO 5: Carry out experiments according to plan
CO 6: Collect and critically interpret experimental data
CO 7: Use data interpretation to refine or formulate new scientific questions or hypotheses
CO 8: Maintain an appropriate and organized laboratory notebook
CO 9: Summarize work and communicate findings through written and oral presentations
